input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the `BindingContext`. The page doesn't know which object to bind against. You need to set it, e.g. in code-behind:

```csharp
public MyPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

**Or** in XAML (add your VM namespace and set it declaratively):

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.MyPage">
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Also make sure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or use `[ObservableProperty]` from the MVVM Community Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The XAML shows bindings but the labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values. This is a classic issue — the `BindingContext` is never set.

Let me give a concise answer.
